questions.question: Which filename will Jekyll recognise as a publishable post in `_posts/`?
questions.explanation: Posts must follow `YYYY-MM-DD-slug.ext` with zero-padded month and day. Files without a date prefix or with single-digit month/day are silently skipped - no warning is emitted.

questions.question: You add a new post to `_drafts/profiling.md` with valid front matter, run `bundle exec jekyll serve`, and the post doesn't appear. What's the simplest explanation and fix?
choices: The Gemfile is missing the `jekyll-drafts` plugin - install it
Drafts are excluded from the default build; re-run with `--drafts`
Drafts must live under `_posts/_drafts/`, not the project root
The file is corrupted; recreate it
questions.explanation: Files in `_drafts/` are deliberately excluded from `jekyll serve` and `jekyll build`. Use `jekyll serve --drafts` to include them during preview, and move the file into `_posts/` with a dated filename to publish.

questions.question: Your post title contains a colon: `Profiling Go: by way of an outage`. Setting `title: Profiling Go: by way of an outage` in front matter causes a YAML parse error. What's the right fix, and why?
choices: Replace the colon with a dash - colons are forbidden in titles
Quote the value: `title: "Profiling Go: by way of an outage"` - colons inside unquoted strings confuse YAML's key/value parser
Move the title into the file body - front matter cannot hold titles
Switch the Markdown engine away from kramdown
questions.explanation: YAML treats `:` followed by a space as a key/value separator, so an unquoted title with a colon parses ambiguously. Wrapping the value in double quotes tells YAML it's a single string.
